input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Check what's available
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Install latest .NET 8 SDK (or 9, adjust as needed)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --accept-source-agreements

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# See available workloads
dotnet workload search

# Install MAUI (pulls in android, ios stubs, etc.)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows App SDK / WinUI3 requires VS build tools:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --accept-source-agreements

# Or if you only want build tools (no IDE):
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools

# Then add required components via the installer or CLI:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  modify --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --passive --norestart
```

## 4. Install OpenJDK (Android toolchain needs it)

```powershell
# Discover available versions
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK

# Install JDK 17 (required by current Android Gradle)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Verify
java -version
```

## 5. Install Android SDK & Emulator

```powershell
# Install Android Studio (easiest way to get SDK manager)
winget install Google.AndroidStudio

# --- OR manage SDK manually via cmdline-tools ---

# Download command-line tools
$sdkRoot = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest"

# After extracting cmdline-tools to $sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest:
$sdkmanager = "$sdkRoot\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# Discover available platforms and build-tools
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select-Object -First 10
& $sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools;"    | Select-Object -First 10

# Install what MAUI needs (adjust API level to latest stable)
& $sdkmanager --install `
  "platforms;android-34" `
  "build-tools;34.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& $sdkmanager --licenses   # type 'y' to each prompt
```

## 6. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Persist ANDROID_HOME
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")

# Add platform-tools to PATH
$path = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "$path;$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk\platform-tools", "User")

# Reload in current session
$env:ANDROID_HOME = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
```

## 7. Create an Android Emulator AVD

```powershell
$avdmanager = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"

# List available device definitions
& $avdmanager list device | Select-String "Name:"

# Create AVD
& $avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_API34" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" -d "pixel"
```

## 8. Enable Windows Developer Mode

```powershell
# Required for Windows target sideloading
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /d 1
```

## 9. Verify Everything

```powershell
# Create and build a test project for both targets
dotnet new maui -n MauiSmokeTest
cd MauiSmokeTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on Android emulator
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_API34 &
dotnet build -f net8.0-android -t:Install -t:Run
```

## 10. Discover Versions Dynamically (cheat sheet)

```powershell
dotnet --list-sdks                          # installed .NET SDKs
dotnet workload list                        # installed workloads
& $sdkmanager --list --installed            # installed Android components
winget list Microsoft.DotNet.SDK            # installed SDK via winget
java -version                               # JDK version
```

---

**Key version tips:** Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if you install .NET 9. Replace `android-34` / `build-tools;34.0.0` with whatever `sdkmanager --list` shows as latest stable. The MAUI workload automatically pulls compatible Android bindings for your SDK version.
